title: Procurement Planning
description: Quiz covering procurement planning, make-or-buy decisions, supplier selection, procurement risks and project procurement strategies.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of procurement planning?
options: To determine what should be purchased, when it should be purchased, and from whom
To reduce the number of project stakeholders
To eliminate project risks
To create the project schedule
questions.explanation: Procurement planning determines what the project needs to acquire externally, when it is needed, and how it should be obtained.

questions.question: Procurement refers to:
options: Acquiring goods, services or works from external suppliers
Recruiting internal employees
Managing project meetings
Creating the Work Breakdown Structure
questions.explanation: Procurement is the process of obtaining products or services from external organizations.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of procurement?
options: Hiring an external engineering consultant
Assigning work to an internal employee
Updating the project schedule
Holding a stakeholder meeting
questions.explanation: Hiring an external consultant is an example of procuring external expertise.

questions.question: What is the first major decision made during procurement planning?
options: The make-or-buy decision
Selecting the cheapest supplier
Approving project scope
Creating the project charter
questions.explanation: Project managers first determine whether work should be performed internally or purchased from an external supplier.

questions.question: What does the make-or-buy decision evaluate?
options: Whether work should be completed internally or outsourced
Whether the project should continue
Whether stakeholders approve the project
Whether project quality should be reduced
questions.explanation: The make-or-buy decision compares performing work internally with purchasing it externally.

questions.question: Why is procurement planning important?
options: Because many projects depend on external suppliers to deliver critical resources
Because procurement automatically reduces project costs
Because procurement eliminates all project risks
Because procurement replaces project scheduling
questions.explanation: Many projects rely on suppliers, making procurement planning essential for successful delivery.

questions.question: Which resource would most likely require procurement?
options: A specialized offshore installation vessel
An internal team meeting
The project manager's weekly report
The project schedule
questions.explanation: Large specialized assets are commonly obtained through external suppliers.

questions.question: Which factor should NOT normally influence a make-or-buy decision?
options: The favorite color of the project manager
Internal expertise
Available capacity
Project cost
questions.explanation: Project decisions should be based on business factors rather than personal preferences.

questions.question: A company decides to outsource software security testing because it lacks internal expertise. This is an example of:
options: A make-or-buy decision
Project crashing
Stakeholder analysis
Scope verification
questions.explanation: Choosing an external specialist instead of developing internal capability is a make-or-buy decision.

questions.question: A project manager wants to avoid delays caused by late supplier deliveries. Which project activity is most helpful?
options: Planning procurement early
Reducing project scope
Ignoring supplier lead times
Waiting until construction begins before contacting suppliers
questions.explanation: Early procurement planning helps account for supplier lead times and reduces the likelihood of schedule delays.

questions.question: A project manager is comparing two suppliers. Supplier A is cheaper, while Supplier B has an excellent delivery record and consistently delivers high-quality products. What should the project manager consider first?
options: Which supplier provides the greatest overall value to the project
Which supplier has the shortest company name
Which supplier is located furthest away
Which supplier submitted their proposal first
questions.explanation: Procurement decisions should consider overall value, including quality, reliability and performance—not just purchase price.

questions.question: Why is selecting the lowest-priced supplier not always the best procurement decision?
options: Lower-priced suppliers may create higher costs through delays, poor quality or rework
The cheapest supplier is legally prohibited from winning contracts
Low prices always indicate fraud
Projects should always select the most expensive supplier
questions.explanation: The lowest purchase price may lead to greater total project costs if quality or delivery performance is poor.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a procurement risk?
options: A supplier fails to deliver critical equipment on time
A project team completes work ahead of schedule
Stakeholders approve the project budget
The project manager finishes the risk register
questions.explanation: Supplier delays are one of the most common procurement risks and can directly affect the project schedule.

questions.question: A project depends on a specialized machine with a six-month delivery time. What should the project manager do?
options: Include the long lead time in the procurement plan and order early
Wait until construction begins before placing the order
Ignore the delivery schedule
Purchase the machine after project completion
questions.explanation: Long lead-time items should be identified early so they do not delay the project.

questions.question: Which supplier characteristic is LEAST useful when evaluating procurement proposals?
options: The supplier owner's favorite sports team
Technical capability
Financial stability
Past delivery performance
questions.explanation: Supplier evaluation should focus on objective business criteria rather than personal characteristics.

questions.question: Why should procurement activities be integrated with the overall project schedule?
options: Because materials and services must be available when project activities require them
Because procurement always determines the project budget
Because suppliers create the Work Breakdown Structure
Because procurement replaces risk management
questions.explanation: Purchasing activities must align with the project schedule to prevent delays caused by missing resources.

questions.question: A project team chooses to manufacture a component internally even though an external supplier offers a lower price. Which reason best justifies this decision?
options: The company wants to protect confidential technology or maintain strategic control
The project manager prefers internal meetings
External suppliers are never allowed
Internal production always costs less
questions.explanation: Make-or-buy decisions consider strategic factors such as confidentiality, intellectual property and long-term capabilities—not only cost.

questions.question: Which procurement strategy best reduces the impact of a potential supplier failure?
options: Identifying qualified backup suppliers during procurement planning
Waiting until the supplier fails before making a plan
Ordering everything from a single unknown supplier
Ignoring supplier financial health
questions.explanation: Having approved alternative suppliers is an effective way to reduce procurement risk.

questions.question: A supplier offers the lowest bid but has a history of missing deadlines. Another supplier charges slightly more but consistently delivers on time. Which supplier would most experienced project managers likely choose?
options: The supplier offering the best overall value and reliability
The cheapest supplier regardless of performance
The supplier with the newest website
Either supplier, since delivery performance is irrelevant
questions.explanation: Reliable delivery often provides greater long-term value than a slightly lower purchase price.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of procurement planning from a project management perspective?
options: To ensure external resources are available at the right time, cost and quality to support successful project delivery
To purchase as many products as possible
To eliminate all project risks
To avoid working with external suppliers
questions.explanation: Procurement planning is about ensuring the project receives the right resources at the right time while balancing cost, quality and risk.

questions.question: A project manager discovers that a critical supplier has entered financial difficulties shortly before a major delivery. What is the best course of action?
options: Activate contingency plans and evaluate alternative qualified suppliers
Ignore the issue until the delivery date arrives
Cancel the entire project immediately
Continue as if nothing has changed
questions.explanation: Procurement planning should include contingency measures for supplier failures, allowing the project to respond before major delays occur.

questions.question: A project team purchases specialized equipment six months before it is needed. Why might this be a good procurement decision?
options: The equipment has a long lead time that could otherwise delay the project
The warehouse has extra storage space
The project manager enjoys ordering equipment early
Early purchasing always reduces project costs
questions.explanation: Long lead-time items are often ordered well in advance to ensure they are available when project activities begin.

questions.question: Which project would most likely require extensive procurement planning?
options: The construction of an offshore wind farm involving multiple international suppliers
A one-hour internal team meeting
A project manager updating a Gantt chart
A weekly project status presentation
questions.explanation: Large engineering projects rely heavily on external suppliers and require detailed procurement planning.

questions.question: Why should procurement risks be identified before contracts are signed?
options: To develop mitigation strategies before problems affect the project
To increase project costs
To reduce the number of suppliers
To eliminate the need for supplier evaluations
questions.explanation: Identifying procurement risks early allows the project team to prepare contingency plans before issues arise.

questions.question: A supplier offers excellent quality but consistently delivers several weeks late. Another supplier has slightly lower quality but always delivers on time. What should the project manager do?
options: Evaluate which supplier provides the greatest overall value based on project priorities
Always choose the highest quality supplier
Always choose the cheapest supplier
Choose randomly because both suppliers are acceptable
questions.explanation: Supplier selection depends on project priorities such as schedule, quality, cost and overall business value.

questions.question: A project sponsor insists on selecting the cheapest supplier despite significant quality concerns. What should the project manager do?
options: Explain the long-term risks and potential total costs associated with the decision
Accept the decision without discussion
Ignore the sponsor's request
Reduce the project's quality standards
questions.explanation: Project managers should provide decision-makers with information about procurement risks and life-cycle costs before final decisions are made.

questions.question: Which statement best reflects the relationship between procurement planning and project scheduling?
options: Poor procurement planning can delay project activities even when the schedule itself is well developed
Procurement planning has no influence on the project schedule
Scheduling always occurs after procurement is complete
Procurement planning replaces schedule management
questions.explanation: Even an excellent project schedule cannot be executed if critical materials or services are unavailable.

questions.question: An organization decides to perform work internally even though outsourcing would cost slightly less. Which factor could justify this decision?
options: Maintaining critical expertise, protecting intellectual property or supporting long-term strategy
The internal team has fewer meetings
Internal employees prefer the work
External suppliers are never reliable
questions.explanation: Strategic considerations often outweigh short-term cost savings in make-or-buy decisions.

questions.question: Which statement best summarizes effective supplier selection?
options: Suppliers should be evaluated using multiple criteria including cost, quality, capability, reliability and risk
The lowest price should always determine the winner
Only technical capability matters
Supplier selection should be based primarily on personal relationships
questions.explanation: Professional procurement evaluates suppliers using multiple objective criteria to maximize project value.

questions.question: What is the most important goal of procurement planning in project management?
options: To ensure the project receives the right goods and services at the right time, quality and cost while supporting successful project delivery
To purchase everything as early as possible
To minimize communication with suppliers
To eliminate all procurement activities from the project
questions.explanation: Successful procurement planning aligns external resources with the project's objectives, balancing cost, quality, schedule and risk.
